tool_args.label: Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) Program

tool_args.label: Paratransit Enterprise Technology System
tool_args.raw_text: Contract for Paratransit technology solution; new enterprise technology system for Paratransit Access-A-Ride operations

payload.description: New enterprise technology system replacing existing RTS/CAD and AVLM systems for Paratransit Access-A-Ride operations, including customer management, reservations, scheduling, dispatching, real-time vehicle tracking and billing/reconciliation

payload.description: Hayden AI Inc. awarded Modification 2 for ACE expansion including bus stop and double-parked vehicle enforcement and up to 1,000 additional ACE systems

tool_args.label: Seon ACE cost savings vs Hayden for 2026-2027
tool_args.raw_text: operating the Seon system on 1,000 buses with five enforcement types will save the Authority $27,407,500 versus operating the Hayden system on 1,023 buses with only three enforcement types from 2026 and 2027

payload.description: Projected savings from operating Seon ACE system vs Hayden ACE system on 1,000/1,023 buses for 2026-2027

tool_args.label: Seon option period cost savings vs Hayden 2028-2033
tool_args.raw_text: If all options to extend both contracts through the year 2033 are exercised, the Seon contract will cost $43,872,228 less during the six-year option period

payload.description: Projected savings from Seon contract vs Hayden during six-year option period (2028-2033)

tool_args.label: Total projected ACE savings through competition 2026-2033
tool_args.raw_text: By cultivating competition, the Authority is projected to save $71,279,728 on the 1,000 systems being purchased from Seon for ACE enforcement from 2026 through 2033

payload.description: Total projected savings through competition on Seon ACE enforcement systems, 2026-2033

tool_args.label: Total September 2025 procurement agenda expenditure
tool_args.raw_text: 3 actions for a proposed estimated expenditure of $330.7M

payload.description: Total proposed estimated expenditure for September 2025 Procurement Agenda
Hayden AI awarded initial three-year contract for ABLE services on 300 buses in estimated amount of $19,655,898
Seon awarded three-year contract for ABLE services on 1,000 buses in estimated amount of $58,405,780
Declaration of Immediate Operating Need for ABLE (Automated Bus Lane Enforcement) systems
NYC Transit with NYC DOT permitted to conduct limited ABLE proof-of-concept with Zenco Systems, Ltd.
Hayden awarded Modification 1 for $63,477,161, adding 723 ABLE systems for total of 1,023 systems, extending through December 2027

Following legislative approval to monitor and ticket vehicles obstructing bus stops and double-parked vehicles, DOB changed project name from ABLE to ACE (Automated Camera Enforcement) and updated scope of work
